function.name: add_rule
function.description: Add a rule node to the rulegraph rule graph. Use this to load a game rule from a rulebook into the graph so it can be queried and arbitrated.

rule_id.description: Unique identifier for this rule, e.g. 'PHB.attack_roll' or 'PF2.action.strike'.

text.description: Full text of the rule as it appears in the rulebook.

node_type.description: Semantic type: 'mechanic' for deterministic rules, 'narrative' for flavor/interpretation, 'formula' for numeric calculations.

source.description: Source book or document, e.g. 'D&D SRD 5.1' or 'Pathfinder 2e CRB'.

db.description: Path to the rulegraph SQLite database file.

function.name: query_rules
function.description: Arbitrate a natural-language question against the rulegraph rule graph. Returns a structured ArbitrationResult with the answer, tier (determinate/indeterminate/unknown), confidence, provenance (which rules were used), and any contradictions detected.

question.description: Natural-language question about game rules, e.g. 'How do I make an attack roll?' or 'What is difficult terrain?'

db.description: Path to the rulegraph SQLite database file.

function.name: arbitrate
function.description: Same as query_rules but also saves the result to the database for later audit. Returns the full ArbitrationResult including tier classification, provenance chain, confidence score, and any rule contradictions.

db.description: Path to the rulegraph SQLite database file.

function.name: list_rules
function.description: List all rules in the rulegraph rule graph, optionally filtered by tag or node type. Useful for browsing what rules are loaded.

node_type.description: Filter rules by this node type (e.g. 'mechanic', 'narrative').

db.description: Path to the rulegraph SQLite database file.
